MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-30-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jasun C. Molinelli, Esq. of ARCHER NORRIS (925-930-6600), appearing for defendant / moving party Moore Bros.
Oren Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Orman Smith, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT MOORE BROS.' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and the tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Moore Bros.' motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that plaintiff was exposed to respirable fibers from an asbestos-containing product supplied by defendant. Plaintiff's responses to contention discovery served by defendant state that plaintiff has knowledge that he was exposed to asbestos-containing welding materials, including asbestos gloves and welding rods, supplied by defendant. The excerpts of plaintiff's deposition testimony submitted with the moving papers do not show that plaintiff lacks such knowledge. Furthermore, at his deposition plaintiff testified that Andy Thompson and Tom Boyd might have knowledge about plaintiff's work 'as it relates to Graybar or Moore Brothers' (1225: 2-8) yet defendant's separate statement contains no reference to either Mr. Thompson or Mr. Boyd, much less any evidence that these men lack knowledge that plaintiff was exposed to respirable fibers from an asbestos-containing product supplied by defendant. Defendant is admonished that reference to 'Exhibits A through D', without any greater specificity, as supporting evidence in its separate statement (see purported undisputed material facts 9, 12 and 13) is insufficient and poses significant burdens on opposing counsel and the court in evaluating whether any purported undisputed fact is in fact undisputed. Even if defendant satisfied its burden of production, plaintiff's declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to respirable fibers from an asbestos-containing product supplied by defendant. Order signed in open court. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7707. =(220/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Linde, Inc. To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-13-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Timothy C. Connor, Esq. of TUCKER ELLIS & WEST (415-617-2209), appearing for defendant Rockwell

Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff Orman Smith

RULING:

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Linde Inc.'s motion to compel further responses to requests for admission is granted. The requests are relevant on Proposition 51 allocation and the number of requests is justified by the complexity inherent in multi-party asbestos cases. Plaintiff must admit or deny whether asbestos fibers attributable to the entities named in the requests were a substantial factor in causing plaintiff's alleged asbestos-related disease or state that he lacks sufficient information to admit or deny. If the entity is a bankruptcy trust, the request shall be construed as asking for information about the bankrupt entity, not the trust. No award for sactions. Judge: Harold Kahn, Not Reported. =(220/HEK)
